description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing at the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by four black metal hoops or bands—two near the top and bottom edges, and two around the midsection. The wood has a weathered, natural brown appearance with visible grain and subtle vertical lines, suggesting age and use. One side of the barrel is marked with the word 'WHISKY' in faded white paint, indicating its likely use for storing or aging spirits. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with no visible openings or spouts. The overall look is robust and utilitarian, suitable for storage or decorative purposes in urban or rustic settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops and faded 'WHISKY' lettering on one side. The surface shows natural wood grain and signs of age.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the side, showing the wood grain and all four black metal hoops, with no visible markings.
description_view_1: This view reveals a faint white scuff or scratch on the wood, with the metal hoops and wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_2: The front of the barrel displays the faded 'WHISKY' label in white paint, making this the most distinctive side.
description_view_3: Another side view similar to view 0, with no visible markings or labels, just the wood and metal hoops.
